name: What kind of windows are best for energy efficiency in the Bay Area coastal climate?
headline: What kind of windows are best for energy efficiency in the Bay Area coastal climate?

mainEntity.name: What kind of windows are best for energy efficiency in the Bay Area coastal climate?
mainEntity.text: What kind of windows are best for energy efficiency in the Bay Area coastal climate?

acceptedAnswer.text: For homes in Pacifica, Daly City, San Bruno, and nearby cities, **double-pane windows with low-E glass and insulated frames** usually offer the best energy efficiency for the cost. These windows help keep warm air inside on chilly coastal nights and reduce heat entering during sunny days. Over time, they can lower heating and power bills and make rooms more comfortable. Look for windows with a good U-factor (how well the window keeps heat from escaping) and a low solar heat gain coefficient (how much heat from sunlight enters). You don't need to know all the technical details, but ask for "energy-efficient" windows appropriate for your coastal climate. A mistake many people make is focusing only on the glass and ignoring the frame and installation. Poorly insulated frames or sloppy installation can let air and moisture through, canceling out the benefits of good glass. Also, beware of overpaying for features that don’t matter much in this area, like extremely dark tint if you don’t have strong direct sun. Seal around the window properly, and consider curtains or shades to add another layer of insulation. The combination of better windows and good installation is what really saves energy—not just the label on the product box.
